div.head: 位置

list.item: G區，為北岩西起第二窟。窟底距現今路面約7米（ ； ）。
f壁方向：148°。

div.ab: 與西側毗鄰的1號窟之間隔一道135釐米寬的外立面，d壁東距臥佛足部約9米。

div.head: 形制
div.p: 敞口方形窟，雙層窟口（ ）。1號窟和2號窟共用的外頂超出內窟口約50釐米（）。窟室平面近方形，前窄後寬； e壁略內凹；窟頂由口部向後部略微下傾，大體仍為平頂。

figure.head: 2號窟刻文區概覽，自右往左依次為d壁、e壁和f壁
Overview of the fields of script in cave 2, on walls d, e, and f, from right to left

list.item: d壁：寬205釐米，前部高265釐米，後部高267釐米。
e壁：寬221釐米，中心點處窟高265釐米，中心點距窟口230釐米。
f壁：寬207釐米，前部高260釐米，後部高260釐米。
雙層窟口：內窟口寬206釐米；外窟口東、西兩側分別較內窟口寬18、13釐米，兩側現存深度分別為60、64-80釐米。

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 窟口殘損，目前已用混凝土從三面（左右兩側及頂部）加固。 混凝土填充了內外窟口之間的長方形內凹，並擴展至窟內東西兩側壁底部，長度分別在130 （東）、140（西）釐米左右。 窟內原地面比現今高出10釐米左右，後來由於風化而降至目前高度，現已被混凝土覆蓋抹平。 為排出窟室後部的積水（水泥地面後部較前部略低），地面新近鑿出一條寬約7釐米的凹槽， 距f壁大致80釐米，一直延伸至窟口處。洞窟後部有泥土累積，其上生長著低矮植物。
窟頂前部岩石呈片狀剝落，暴露出的部份為黃色。而同樣存在小片岩石剝落現象的後部窟頂，則由於潮濕的緣故，呈現棕灰色。 d壁後部靠近e壁的拐角處有兩個長方形人工鑿孔， 距離地面的高度分別為100-104、150-156釐米。

list.item: 打磨面面積約為228×195釐米，上緣不規則，約自窟頂下方12釐米處開始，止於現今地面上方約27釐米處； 其距窟口之準確距離現已無法確定，距e壁約7-10釐米。
刻文區面積約為199（前部）至205（後部）×181釐米，上緣距窟頂23-31釐米，下緣距現今地面約41釐米； 兩側分別距窟口約6釐米、e壁11-21釐米。從第56行開始，每行之起頭較此前高出6釐米左右。

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 保存情況不一。高度在27（後部）至67（前部）釐米以下的壁面殘失，現用水泥修補抹平。從窟口95至158釐米高的範圍內開始， 有一橫向的箭頭狀區域，長度約為170釐米，其內字跡幾乎完全漫漶。該區域在大小上不及對面f壁上的風化區，亦不及1號窟d壁與f壁近窟口處的三角形剝落區。另有一條8至15（近窟口）釐米寬的水平裂縫， 在距地面約203釐米高的地方穿過第1至41行刻經。到第77行時，這條裂縫再次出現，高度約為186釐米， 並一直延伸至d壁結束。隨後它又繼續穿過e壁與f壁。

row.cell: ［妙法蓮華經提婆達多品第十二］，現已不可見；據2號窟e壁第20行補出。 《妙法蓮華經》卷四第十二品《提婆達多品》自此開始。
［……］太子［……］；《法華經》卷四第十二品《提婆達多品》開頭可見之二字。
［妙法蓮］華經勸持品第十三；表示《法華經》卷四第十三品《勸持品》之開始。
妙法蓮華經卷第［四］；表示《法華經》第四卷結束，同時也相當於第十三品《勸持品》的結束。
［妙法蓮華經安樂行品第十四］，之下應有小號“五”字； 現已不可見；依1號窟f壁第48行的經品名寫刻形式類推補出。 為《法華經》第十四品《安樂行品》之始，亦為經文第五卷之始。
妙法［蓮］華經從地［踊］出品第［十五］；表示《法華經》卷五第十五品《從地踊出品》的開始。
［妙法蓮華經如］來壽量品第十六；表示《法華經》卷五第十六品《如來壽量品》的開始。
［妙］法蓮華經分［別］功［德品第十七］；d壁最後的刻字，表示《法華經》卷五第十七品《分別功德品》自此開始。

list.item: 打磨面面積約為217×204釐米，自窟頂下方15至21釐米處開始，止於現今地面上方28至32釐米處； 距d壁6至10釐米、f壁6至13釐米。
刻文區面積約為210×197釐米，上緣距窟頂16至21釐米，下緣距現今地面約37釐米； 兩側分別距d壁10至13釐米、f壁10至14釐米。

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 保存不良。高度在25釐米以下的壁面已腐朽，現被水泥覆蓋。 約自地面上方76釐米處始，直至98釐米高處，有一條殘損嚴重的水平風化帶，其內已無文字。 其下方的經文亦無法辨識。而在該區域之上，一直到大約170釐米高的地方，可見塊狀分佈的文字。 貫穿三壁的水平裂縫在e壁寬2至7釐米，大致位於現窟室地面上方190釐米處。距f壁40釐米的地方，沿該縫隙有滲水現象。

row.cell: 爾時大會，聞佛說壽命劫數長遠如是；e壁開頭之刻經，亦為《法華經》卷五第十七品《分別功德品》之正文開頭。
［妙法蓮華經卷第五］；現已不可見，根據1號窟f壁第48行的第三卷卷尾標記“妙法蓮［華］經卷第三”類推補出。 表示經文第五卷至此結束，同時也是《法華經》第十七品《分別功德品》的結束。
妙法蓮華經隨喜功德品第十八；其後有一較小的“六”字；為《法華經》第十八品《隨喜功德品》之始， 亦為經文第六卷之始。
妙法蓮華［經法師功德］品第［十九］；缺失文字依2號窟e壁第20行類推補出。表示《法華經》卷六第十九品《法師功德品》自此開始。
妙法蓮華經常不輕菩薩品第廿；為《法華經》卷六第二十品《常不輕菩薩品》之開始。
……佛滅後，聞如是經，勿生……。佛 ［疾成佛道］；《法華經》卷六第二十品《常不輕菩薩品》可見的最後幾個刻字。
［妙法蓮華經如來神力品第廿一］；不可見；依2號窟e壁第20行類推補出。為《法華經》卷六第二十一品《如來神力品》之開始。
［……］地踊［……］；第二十一品中可清楚辨識出的頭兩個字。
妙法蓮［華］經囑［累品第廿二］；表示《法華經》卷六第二十二品《囑累品》之開始。
……聲……切世……；《法華經》卷六第二十二品《囑累品》於壁面上最後可見之數字。
［妙法蓮華經藥王菩薩本事品第廿三］；現已不可見；據2號窟e壁第20行類推補出。表示《法華經》卷六第二十三品《藥王菩薩本事品》自此開始。
妙［法蓮華經卷第］六；本行最後，表示經文第六卷至此結束， 同時也是《法華經》第二十三品《藥王菩薩本事品》的結束。

list.item: 打磨面面積約為221×196釐米，約自窟頂下方11釐米處開始，止於現今地面上方28釐米處； 內側距e壁10至11釐米，根據窟口處留存的上部壁面判斷，打磨面很可能一直延伸至窟口， 同1號窟f壁情況一致。
刻文區面積約為214×148釐米，上緣距窟頂15至22釐米，下緣距現今地面約31釐米； 兩側分別距e壁15至17釐米、現窟口約42釐米。

div.head: 保存狀況
div.p: 保存不良。高度在24至48釐米以下的底部壁面完全殘朽，已由水泥覆蓋。 自窟口開始，有一塊與窟口等高的風化區，向窟内延伸120釐米後，止於地面上方215釐米處， 該範圍內的壁面表層大面積剝蝕，深度達4至8釐米。情況與1號窟中d壁和f壁（面積略小）類似。 在這一範圍内，僅下部一塊凸向窟口的半圓形區域保存文字。 位於壁面左下角的文字，如同被水沖刷過一樣，刻字的位置尚可判斷，但字形已很難辨認。 距e壁約90釐米的地方，有一道形狀不規則的裂縫從窟頂向下延伸至地面上方165釐米處。 另有一條水平裂縫自e壁延伸過來，在現窟室地面上方195釐米處進入f壁。 之後它繼續延伸，穿過文字區，造成經文中斷，最後終止於距離窟口約50釐米、距現地面215釐米高處。

row.cell: 妙法蓮華經妙音菩薩品第廿四；根據2號窟e壁第20行的經品名寫刻形式推測，其後應有一小字“七”。 表示《法華經》第二十四品《妙音菩薩品》之開始，同時也是經文第七卷之開始。
妙法蓮華經觀世音菩薩普門品第廿［五］；為《法華經》卷七第二十五品《觀世音菩薩普門品》之始。
妙法蓮華經陀羅尼品第廿六；為《法華經》卷七第二十六品《陀羅尼品》之始。
妙法蓮華經妙莊嚴王本事［品］第［廿七］；表示《法華經》卷七第二十七品《妙莊嚴王本事品》自此開始。
妙法蓮華經普賢菩薩勸發品第廿八；表示《法華經》卷七第二十八品《普賢菩薩勸發品》自此開始。
妙［法蓮華經卷第七］；風化字跡據1號窟f壁第48行補出。表示經文第七卷結束，亦即第二十八品《普賢菩薩勸發品》之結束， 同時也是整部《妙法蓮華經》的結束。

div.head: 討論（1、2號窟）
div.bibl:
胡文和、李官智 20, 25
胡文和與李官智是最早判定二窟所刻經文為《妙法蓮華經》的學者，並給出其中五品（第十五、十八、二十三、二十四、二十五品）的品名。

彭家勝 6
彭家勝給出二窟的高、寬、深三維尺寸，進而計算出窟室體積，分別是1號窟13.29立方米、2號窟12.836 立方米。他判定二窟內僅刊刻一部《妙法蓮華經》，並辨識出其中五品的品名， 以及它們所處的壁面位置（實際上只有第八、十八、二十四和二十五品四品，《觀音普門品》出現兩次）。稱六壁經文均保存“較好”。

曹丹 51
曹丹記錄了《妙法蓮華經》部份卷品在窟室壁面上的大致位置。

胡文和 8
胡文和記錄了兩座洞窟的三維尺寸（同彭家勝，1988年，第6頁），不過並未計算其體積。他辨識出部份經品名稱，並標明其大致壁面位置：《妙法蓮華經五百弟子受記品（第八）》位於1號窟f壁。《妙法蓮華經》第十五品位於2號窟d壁；第十八品位於e壁；第二十三至二十五品位於f壁。通過比對，判定此《妙法蓮華經》為鳩摩羅什譯本。

李良、鄧之金 38
李良和鄧之金認為這兩座洞窟開鑿於唐代。他們記錄了洞窟的大致位置、形制和尺寸。 測量了文字區，並統計出各壁經文字數：1號窟，d壁9,170字，e壁12,407字，f壁9,317字；2號窟，d壁7,797字，e壁9,709字，f壁9,072字。測得的經文字徑為1.5至2.0釐米。
他們描述兩窟内的經文均保存“較好”，但同時還提到窟室壁面曾於1982年進行過化學封護。

李琛妍 66-68
李琛妍刊載了一張由作者本人拍攝的1、2號窟外立面照片（第67頁，圖24），同時還有一幅臥佛前木結構建築的假想復原圖 （第48頁，圖14），該圖顯示了臥佛同兩座經窟的位置關係。判斷這兩座洞窟開鑿於8世紀上半葉。
作者認為完整呈現在巨型造像近旁的、鳩摩羅什翻譯的《妙法蓮華經》， 提供了關於如何在佛陀崇拜及供養活動中使用種種實物的最權威實例。
此外，作者還持以下觀點：《妙法蓮華經》可以看作臥佛院形成的源頭，這同臥佛扮演的定義 “位置”參數、引發相應“回應”的角色 並無二致。臥佛和《法華經》共同界定了佛陀進入涅槃後的時間框架，並依託涅槃主題喚起世人的關注──佛教如何在佛陀 離開人世的時代中存續。山谷對面的其他佛教刻經則對此作出回應，試圖去合理化並維護佛教信仰的持久性。

成都考古發現 2006 354、403-404
這份報告較為詳細地描述了兩座洞窟的位置與形制。認為它們是同一時期規劃而開鑿的一組刻經雙窟。
當時1號窟下方建有一座庫房，無法攀至該窟內進行調查。因此該報告僅提供了2號窟及其內文字區的測量數據，並記錄各壁保存狀況如下：d壁上半部保存較好，中部略有風化，底部壁面斷失，經文已不存；e壁僅存上部刻經，下部風化殆盡；f壁窟口中部風化較嚴重，上部較好。此外，文中還提到d壁後部有晚期開鑿的兩個孔洞；窟室原地平較現存地面高出10 釐米左右。
報告中判定1、2號窟內刊刻有一部完整的《妙法蓮華經》，並根據安岳縣文物局庫存的1號窟經文拓片和2號窟窟壁上的刻經，給出該窟各卷品名稱及其在壁面上的具體位置。

曹丹、于春 第10-11、16、18、28-31頁
曹丹和于春收錄了《妙法蓮華經》的部份拓片（第0009至0011、0046至0048號拓片）。
測量了文字區，並統計出各壁現存經文字數，所得數據如下：1號窟，d壁現存1,315字；e壁僅存751字；f 壁現存3,001字。
2號窟，d壁尚存7,308字；e壁現存3,906字；f 壁現存3,529字。
此外，本文還簡要介紹了《妙法蓮華經》的主旨要義和譯者鳩摩羅什之的生平與主要譯經， 並且列出該經各卷品名稱。通過比較，作者認為臥佛院刊刻的《法華經》並未從第一卷開始逐卷刻出， 這與刻經供養人的選擇有關。

雷玉華、王劍平、傅成金 98
這兩座洞窟位於大佛足部以西，處於崖面上較次的位置，應晚於大佛開鑿之際。

div.head: Location

list.item: Second cave from the west in the northern cliff, section G; ca. 7 m above the present walkway at the foot of the cliff (fig. and fig. ).
Direction of wall f: 148˚.

div.ab: Separated from cave 1 to the west by a facade 135 cm wide. Distance to the feet of the Buddha statue in the east ca. 9 m.

div.head: Shape
div.p: Box-shaped with open front; double entrance frame (fig. ). The roof shared by caves 1 and 2 protrudes ca. 50 cm from the solid rock above (fig. ). An almost square ground plan, widening toward the rear; the rear wall e is slightly concave; the ceiling descends slightly toward the rear.

list.item: Wall d: height at front 265, at rear 267 cm, width 205 cm.
Wall e: height at midpoint 265 cm; width 221 cm, distance from midpoint to entrance: 230 cm.
Wall f: height at front 260 cm, at rear 260 cm, width 207 cm.
Width of the entrance opening: 206 cm. Width of reveal, 18 cm (east), 13 cm (west); depth of reveal 60 cm (east), ca. 64-80 cm (west).

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: The door frame has lost its front sections; it is now encased by concrete to stabilize its three sides, including the lintel. The concrete fills the rectangular niches between the inner and outer frame and extends into the cave near the floor for ca. 130 cm (east), and ca. 140 cm (west). The original floor was ca. 10 cm higher than the present one, but it was worn down and has now been covered in concrete. A channel, ca. 7 cm wide, has recently been cut into the modern floor to drain the water from the rear. It runs at ca. 80 cm away from wall f, to the front of the cave. At the rear of the cave, earth has accumulated with low-growing vegetation.

Flat pieces of rock have fallen from front parts of the ceiling, which displays a yellow color. Smaller pieces of rock have also fallen from the rear part which has a grey-brownish color on account of moisture. Two rectangular man-made holes appear in the rear of wall d, near the corner with wall e, at heights of 100-104 cm and 150-156 cm.

list.item: A polished area, ca. 228 × 195 cm, with an irregular upper boundary, begins ca. 12 cm below the ceiling; the exact distance to the entrance opening cannot now be established; the polished area ends ca. 7-10 cm from wall e, and ca. 27 cm above the present floor.

The field of script, ca. 199 (front) - 205 (rear) × 181 cm, begins ca. 6 cm from the entrance opening and 23-31 cm below the ceiling. It ends ca. 41 cm above the present floor and 11-21 cm from wall e. Beginning with column 56, the columns rise a further ca. 6 cm by steps.

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: Mixed. The foot of the original wall has disappeared up to a height of ca. 27 cm (rear) - 67 cm (front) and the area has been covered by concrete. A horizontal, arrow-shaped zone, without characters, begins at the entrance opening between 95 cm and 158 cm above the floor, and extends to a point ca. 170 cm inside the cave. This field is not as large as the one on the opposite wall f, or as those on walls d and f in cave 1. A horizontal crack, ca. 203 cm above the present floor and 15 cm (near the entrance) - 8 cm wide, cuts through columns 1-41. This crack resurfaces in column 77, at a height of 186 cm above the present floor, and it then continues to the end of wall d. It further continues on walls e and f.

row.cell: ［妙法蓮華經提婆達多品第十二］, no longer visible; missing characters supplied by ana-logy with cave 2, wall e, column 20. Refers to the beginning of chapter 12, entitled Tipodaduo [Devadatta] pin 提婆達多品, in scroll 4 of the the Lotus Sutra .
[...]太子[...]; first visible characters in the beginning of chapter 12, entitled Tipodaduo [Devadatta] pin 提婆達多品, in scroll 4 of the Lotus Sutra.
［妙法蓮］華經勸持品第十三; refers to the beginning of chapter 13, entitled Quanchi pin 勸持品, in scroll 4 of the Lotus Sutra.
妙法蓮華經卷第［四］; refers to the end of scroll 4, coinciding with the end of chapter 13, entitled Quanchi pin 勸持品, in scroll 4 of the Lotus Sutra.

［妙法蓮華經安樂行品第十四］ Assumed to have been followed by a small character 5, wu 五; no longer visible; missing characters supplied by analogy with cave 1, wall f, column 48. Refers to the beginning of chapter 14, entitled Anlexing pin 安樂行品, coinciding with the beginning of scroll 5 in the Lotus Sutra.
妙法［蓮］華經從地［踊］出品第［十五］; refers to the beginning of chapter 15, entitled Congdi yongchu pin 從地踊出品, in scroll 5 of the Lotus Sutra.
［妙法蓮華經如］來壽量品第十六; refers to the beginning of chapter 16, entitled Rulai shouliang pin 如來壽量品, in scroll 5 of the Lotus Sutra.
［妙］法蓮華經分［別］功［德品第十七］; refers to the beginning of chapter 17, entitled Fenbie gongde pin 分別功德品, in scroll 5 of the Lotus Sutra. These are the last characters on wall d.

list.item: A polished area, ca. 217 × 204 cm, begins 6-10 cm from wall d and 15-21 cm from the ceiling; it ends 28-32 cm above the present floor and 6-13 cm from wall f.
The field of script, ca. 210 × 197 cm, begins 10-13 cm from wall d and 16-21 cm below the ceiling; it ends ca. 37 cm above the present floor and ca. 10-14 cm from wall f.

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: Poor. The foot of the wall has decayed and has been covered by concrete up to a height of ca. 25 cm. A horizontal zone, ca. 76-98 cm above the floor, is damaged and no characters remain. Nor are characters recognizable below that zone. Above this zone, up to ca. 170 cm, patches of characters are visible. The horizontal crack, which runs through all three walls, has in this wall e a width of 2-7 cm and appears ca. 190 cm above the present floor. Water comes slipping along the crack 40 cm from wall f.

row.cell: 爾時大會, 聞佛說壽命劫數長遠如是; the first characters on wall e, which are the beginning of chapter 17, entitled Fenbie gongde pin 分別功德品, in scroll 5 of the Lotus Sutra.

［妙法蓮華經卷第五］; no longer visible; missing characters supplied by analogy with cave 1, wall f, column 48. Refers to the end of scroll 5, coinciding with the end of chapter 17, entitled Fenbie gongde pin 分別功德品, in scroll 5 of the Lotus Sutra.
妙法蓮華經隨喜功德品第十八; followed by a small character 6, liu 六; refers to the beginning of chapter 18, entitled Suixi gongde pin 隨喜功德品, coinciding with the beginning of scroll 6 of the Lotus Sutra.
妙法蓮華［經法師功德］品第［十九］; missing characters supplied by analogy with cave 2, wall e, column 20. Refers to the beginning of chapter 19, entitled Fashi gongde pin 法師功德品, in scroll 6 of the Lotus Sutra.
妙法蓮華經常不輕菩薩品第廿; refers to the beginning of chapter 20, entitled Changbuqing pusa pin 常不輕菩薩品, in scroll 6 of the Lotus Sutra.
...佛滅後，聞如是經，勿生....佛［疾成佛道］; the last visible characters in chapter 20, entitled Changbuqing pusa pin 常不輕菩薩品, in scroll 6 of the Lotus Sutra.

［妙法蓮華經如來神力品第廿一］; no longer visible; missing characters supplied by analogy with cave 2, wall e, column 20. Refers to the beginning of chapter 21, entitled Rulai shenli pin 如來神力品, in scroll 6 of the Lotus Sutra.
[...]地踊[...]; the first two clearly visible characters in this chapter 21.
妙法蓮［華］經囑［累品第廿二］; refers to the beginning of chapter 22, entitled Zhulei pin 囑累品, in scroll 6 of the Lotus Sutra.
...聲...切世...; the last visible characters in chapter 22, entitled Zhulei pin 囑累品, in scroll 6 of the Lotus Sutra.

［妙法蓮華經藥王菩薩本事品第廿三］; no longer visible; missing characters supplied by analogy with cave 2, wall e, column 20. Refers to the beginning of chapter 23, entitled Yaowang pusa benshi pin 藥王菩薩本事品, in scroll 6 of the Lotus Sutra.
Column 93 (the last column on this wall)
妙［法蓮華經卷第］六; the last characters in this column; refers to the end of scroll 6, coinciding with the end of chapter 23, entitled Yaowang pusa benshi pin 藥王菩薩本事品, in scroll 6 of the Lotus Sutra.

list.item: A polished area, ca. 221 × 196 cm, begins 10-11 cm from wall e and 11 cm from the ceiling; it ends 28 cm above the present floor; judging by its upper part, the polished field probably extended all the way to the entrance opening, as on wall f of cave 1.
The field of script, 214 × 148 cm, begins 15-17 cm from wall e and 15-22 cm below the ceiling; it ends ca. 31 cm above the present floor and ca. 42 cm from the present entrance opening.

div.head: State of preservation
div.p: Poor. The foot of the wall has decayed and has been covered by concrete up to a height of ca. 24-48 cm. A damaged area, in which the surface has been eroded to a depth of 4-8 cm, extends from the height of the entire entrance opening ca. 120 cm into the center of the wall and up to a height of 215 cm above the floor. This damage is comparable with the situation in cave 1, on wall d and (somewhat smaller) on wall f. Only in the lower portion of this area are characters left in a half circle area bulging toward the entrance. In the lower rear corner of the wall, the positions of the characters can still be determined, but they appear as if washed out and are hardly recognizable. An irregularly shaped crack reaches down from the ceiling to a point 165 cm above the present floor and ca. 90 cm from wall e. The horizontal crack coming in from wall e, hits wall f at a height of 195 cm above the present floor. It continues with interruptions and ends ca. 50 cm before the entrance opening at a height of 215 cm above the present floor.

row.cell: 妙法蓮華經妙音菩薩品第廿四; assumed to have been followed by a small character 7, qi 七, by analogy with cave 2, wall e, column 20; refers to the beginning of chapter 24, entitled Miaoyin pusa pin 妙音菩薩品, coinciding with the beginning of scroll 7 of the Lotus Sutra.
妙法蓮華經觀世音菩薩普門品第廿［五］; refers to the beginning of chapter 25, entitled Guanshiyin pusa pumen pin 觀世音菩薩普門品, in scroll 7 of the Lotus Sutra.
妙法蓮華經陀羅尼品第廿六; refers to the beginning of chapter 26, entitled Tuoluoni pin 陀羅尼品, in scroll 7 of the Lotus Sutra.
妙法蓮華經妙莊嚴王本事［品］第［廿七］; refers to the beginning of chapter 27, entitled Miaozhuangyan wang benshi pin 妙莊嚴王本事品, in scroll 7 of the Lotus Sutra.
妙法蓮華經普賢菩薩勸發品第廿八; refers to the beginning of chapter 28, entitled Puxian pusa quanfa pin 普賢菩薩勸發品, in scroll 7 of the Lotus Sutra.

妙［法蓮華經卷第七］; missing characters supplied by analogy with cave 1, wall f, column 48. Refers to the end of scroll 7, coinciding with the end of chapter 28, entitled Puxian pusa quanfa pin 普賢菩薩勸發品, coinciding with the end of the entire Lotus Sutra.

note.p: Hu and Li are the first to identify the text in the two caves as the Lotus Sutra in Kumārajīva’s translation, and give the titles of five chapters (15, 18, 23, 24, and 25).

Peng gives measurements of the three dimensions of the caves and computes their volume as 13.29 m³ (cave 1) and 12.836 m³ (cave 2). He identifies the Lotus Sutra as the only text carved in the two caves and the titles of five (one of them twice) chapters, locating them on the walls. He describes the state of preservation on all six walls as “rather good 較好”.
Cao gives the location of some chapters on the walls.

Hu gives measurements of the three dimensions of both caves (on the same lines as 6), but does not compute their volume. He identifies chapter 8, 五百弟子受記品, on wall f, cave 1, locates chapter 15 on wall d, cave 2, chapter 18 on wall e, and chapters 23, 24, 25 on wall f. He identifies the version of the Lotus Sutra as the translation by Kumārajīva.

Li and Deng believe that the two caves were carved in the Tang dynasty. They give location, shape, and measurements of the caves. They also set out the measurements of the fields of script, and state the total number of characters in cave 1 as 9,170 (wall d), 12,407 (wall e), 9,317 (wall f), and the total number of characters in cave 2 as 7,797 (wall d), 9,709 (wall e), 9,072 (wall f). They report that the characters have a diameter of 1.5-2.0 cm.
They describe the state of preservation of the sutra in both caves as “rather good 較好”, and mention that in 1982 a protective chemical coating 化學封護處理保護 was applied to the surfaces of walls.
This report gives a detailed description of the location and shape of the two caves. It is suggested that the twin sutra caves were part of an overall unified plan.

Cave 1 was inaccessible at the time of the field work, because a storehouse had been built below it. Thus this report provides measurements only for cave 2 and its fields of script, and makes observations on the state of preservation: Wall d, lower part already decayed; middle part slightly damaged; upper part well preserved. Wall e, lower part completely eroded; sutras only preserved on the upper part. Wall f: middle part near the entrance severely eroded; upper part rather well preserved. Also mentioned are two holes in wall d, which were added later. The floor is ca. 10 cm lower than the original level.

The report identifies the Lotus Sutra as the only text in two caves and states that it was carved in its entirety. It locates scrolls and chapters in cave 1 based on rubbings in the Anyue Cultural Relics Bureau and on the walls of cave 2 based on on-site inspection.

Lee publishes her photo of caves 1 and 2 (p. 67, fig. 24) and a drawing (p. 48, fig. 14) with a hypothetical reconstruction of the wooden building for the statue of the reclining Buddha and its relation to the two caves. She believes that the two caves were carved in the first half of the eighth century.

In her view, the Lotus Sutra in Kumārajīva’s translation, which is presented in full and situated beside the colossal image, provides an authoritative case for the use of material objects in the devotional practice and worship of the Buddha.

She claims that the Lotus Sutra can be regarded “as the generative source of Wofoyuan, not unlike the role of the reclining Buddha in defining the parameters of a ‘situation’ to which a ‘response’ was made.” The colossal figure and the sutra bracket the time frame after the Buddha’s entering nirvana, and rely on the nirvana theme to call attention to the survival of Buddhism in an age without the Buddha’s presence. The other Buddhist stone sutras on the opposite side of the valley offer a response that seeks to rationalize and uphold the permanence of the Buddhist faith.
Cao and Yu publish rubbings (nos. 0009-0011, 0046-0048) of parts of the Lotus Sutra engraved in two caves.

They give measurements of the fields of script and the number of the characters still extant on each wall in caves 1 and 2 as follows: Cave 1: wall d 1,315 characters extant; wall e 751 characters extant; wall f 3,001 characters extant.
Cave 2: wall d 7,308 characters extant; wall e 3,906 characters extant; wall f 3,529 characters extant.

The authors give a brief introduction to the Lotus Sutra and its translator Kumārajīva. They also list titles of scrolls and chapters of the sutra. They believe that the Lotus Sutra at Wofoyuan was not engraved in its entirety, and that the faithful, who donated money, determined which scrolls or chapters were engraved.
The two caves are situated to the west of the feet of the reclining Buddha. As this is a site in the cliff face that is less desirable, they must have been carved after the colossal statue.
